title: dorstenia barteri var. multiradiata
intro: Dorstenia barteri var. multiradiata, also known as the West African liverwort or Dorstenia, is a plant species in the family Moraceae that is native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimalarial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to aid in the treatment of liver and kidney problems. Dorstenia barteri var. multiradiata can be prepared in herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
